vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present.

cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: All interfaces are named with exact neighboring subsystem labels and DOF.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high temperature range of -20 to +50C.
risk.mit: Implement thermal vias and conductive materials to manage heat.

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through camera window.
risk.mit: Use waterproof seals and gaskets around camera window.

risk.desc: Risk of avionics overheating due to limited cooling.
risk.mit: Increase the number of thermal vias and improve cooling design.

risk.desc: Risk of fuselage deformation under high load conditions.
risk.mit: Strengthen fuselage structure and add reinforcing ribs.

risk.desc: Risk of GPS signal interference due to metal components.
risk.mit: Use non-metallic materials around GPS boom mount.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.name: Nacelle loads to fuselage
physics_paths.path: Loads from wing spar socket transfer through the fuselage spine to the landing gear mounts.

physics_paths.name: Avionics cooling
physics_paths.path: Heat from avionics bay is dissipated through the fuselage shell via thermal vias.

physics_paths.name: GPS signal path
physics_paths.path: GPS signals are received at the front GPS boom mount and routed to the avionics bay.

physics_paths.name: Camera view path
physics_paths.path: Camera view from the belly camera window is transmitted to the avionics bay for processing.

physics_paths.name: Service access
physics_paths.path: Service personnel can access the avionics bay through the service hatch.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to direct sun exposure.

risk.desc: Limited service access for payload/battery maintenance.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

physics_paths.description: Carries rotor thrust loads from nacelle into fuselage spine
Heat from sun exposure is conducted through wing spar to fuselage spine
Service access for payload/battery maintenance through wing spar socket

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install spar socket into fuselage spine first
Align V tail flaps to wing spar axis
Snap fit gear leg into wing spar socket

internal_feature_targets.description: Dual 15mm CFRP spar running the length of the wing
Socket for receiving the wing spar into the fuselage spine
Attachment point for the fuselage spine
Flap for V tail control surface
Control surface for rudder and elevator functions
Mounting point for landing gear leg
Socket for receiving the wing spar into the fuselage spine

integration_opportunities.description: Service access for payload/battery maintenance through wing spar socket
Thermal management through heat sink fins on wing spar
Structural reinforcement through additional ribs and stiffeners

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: H7/h6 fit class for mating interfaces
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM process, orientation for optimal print direction
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: CMM inspection for critical dimensions and fit

verification_focus.description: Verification of load-bearing capacity of wing spar
Verification of thermal expansion and contraction
Verification of accessibility for payload/battery service

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Slide-in li-ion battery pack bay with CG adjustment, BMS and connector access
physics_paths: Load path: battery load transferred to battery bay shell via internal ribs and stiffeners, then to vehicle structure via mounting interfaces.
Flow path: cooling airflow from intake to exhaust ports, managed by internal ducts.
Thermal path: heat generated by battery pack transferred to ambient via thermal channels and fins.
Electrical/data path: battery pack connects to vehicle via connector access, BMS communicates with vehicle via connector access.
Human/service access path: battery bay can be accessed via access cover for servicing and inspection.
integration_opportunities: Battery bay can be integrated with the vehicle structure to ensure proper load transfer and mounting.
Battery bay can be integrated with the avionics bay to ensure proper harness routing and connector access.
Battery bay can be integrated with the battery pack to ensure proper electrical and thermal management.
